1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule

407: ////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
408: 
409: module connectivity_mapping #(
410:     parameter NB_MANAGER      = 4,
411:     parameter NB_SUBORDINATE       = 4,
412:     parameter NB_PRIV_LVL    = 4,   
413:     parameter PRIV_LVL_WIDTH = 4   
414:   )(
415:     input logic [PRIV_LVL_WIDTH-1:0] 	       priv_lvl_i,   
416:     input logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0][NB_PRIV_LVL-1:0] access_ctrl_i,  
417:     
418:     output logic [NB_SUBORDINATE-1:0][NB_MANAGER-1:0]  connectivity_map_o
419:   ); 
420: 
421:     
422:   genvar i,j ; 
423: 
424:   generate
425:     
426:     for (i=0; i<NB_SUBORDINATE; i++)
427:     begin
428:       	for (j=0; j<NB_MANAGER; j++)
429:       	begin
430: 	  assign connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] || ((j==6) && access_ctrl_i[i][7][priv_lvl_i]) ; 
431:       	end  		    		      		
432:     end
433: 
434:   endgenerate 
435: 
436: endmodule

      "obligation_id": "PERM-DBG-001",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "JTAG/DMI debug requester from SimJTAG/dmi_jtag or SimDTM",
      "operation": "read/write/control via DMI debug request/response path",
      "object": "dm_top debug module and downstream debug AXI/core control reachable through debug_req/debug_resp",
      "expected_guard": "Debug/JTAG/DMI requests capable of accessing protected SoC state or controlling the core should be explicitly authorized/authenticated, not merely selected by jtag_enable.",
      "observed_behavior": "The visible harness multiplexes JTAG-derived DMI traffic and SimDTM traffic into dm_top based only on jtag_enable[0]. The selected debug_req_valid/debug_req/debug_resp_ready connect directly to dm_top DMI ports. A jtag_key signal is connected into dmi_jtag, but no visible harness logic checks it or gates debug_req_valid/debug_req with successful authentication. If InclSimDTM is enabled, dmi_req_valid comes directly from SimDTM; it is tied off only in the else branch when InclSimDTM is false.",
      "path": "SimJTAG -> dmi_jtag -> jtag_dmi_req/jtag_req_valid OR SimDTM -> dmi_req/dmi_req_valid; muxed by jtag_enable[0] into debug_req/debug_req_valid; connected to dm_top.dmi_req_i/dmi_req_valid_i; dm_top produces debug AXI master and core debug controls.",
      "state_condition": {
        "jtag_enable": "At tb/ariane_testharness.sv:107-108, jtag_enable is set from plusarg jtag_rbb_enable or defaults to 0. At lines 112-117 it selects JTAG path when jtag_enable[0] is true and SimDTM/DMI path otherwise.",
        "reset_init_done": "init_done is assigned rst_ni at line 105; SimJTAG reset is ~rst_ni at line 123.",
        "handshake": "debug_req_valid/debug_req_ready and debug_resp_valid/debug_resp_ready are passed to dm_top at lines 207-212.",
        "dmi_req_valid_tieoff": "Only tied to '0 in the non-InclSimDTM generate else branch at line 177; default parameter InclSimDTM is 1'b1 at line 21."
      },
      "impact": "External or simulation debug traffic can reach the debug module request interface and potentially trigger debug-module bus accesses or protected state effects without a visible authorization gate in the harness.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:65 declares jtag_key.",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:107-108 initializes jtag_enable from plusarg or default.",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:112-117 mux debug_req_valid, debug_req, and response valid/ready solely on jtag_enable[0].",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:135-153 instantiates dmi_jtag with .jtag_key(jtag_key), DMI request/response ports, and JTAG pins.",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:161-175 instantiates SimDTM with debug_req_valid/debug_req_bits/debug_resp ports.",
        "tb/ariane_testharness.sv:186-212 connects selected DMI request/response directly to dm_top.",
